input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS, targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (currently Xcode 16.x).
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install        # installs CLI tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- Open Xcode → **Settings → Platforms** → download the iOS simulator runtime you need.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the **latest .NET 8 or .NET 9 SDK** (MAUI ships with both; check the [MAUI support policy](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) for the latest supported version).
- Use the **macOS installer (`.pkg`)** for Apple Silicon or Intel as appropriate.
- Verify:
  ```
  dotnet --list-sdks
  ```

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and all dependencies.
- Discover available workloads: `dotnet workload search`
- Verify installed workloads: `dotnet workload list`

## 4. Install JDK (for Android)

The MAUI Android workload bundles a JDK, but if you need one explicitly:

- **Source:** [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) — **JDK 17** is the recommended version.
- Alternatively, the Android workload will auto-provision one. Check with:
  ```
  dotnet build -t:GetAndroidSdkInfo
  ```

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload auto-provisions the Android SDK. To manage it manually:

- **Source:** [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) (optional but useful for the AVD manager and SDK manager UI).
- If you skip Android Studio, the `maui-android` workload handles SDK downloads. First build triggers provisioning.
- Set `ANDROID_HOME` if needed:
  ```
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx"
  ```
- Accept licenses:
  ```
  dotnet build -t:AcceptAndroidSdkLicenses
  ```

## 6. Choose an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit** | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com/) + install the **C# Dev Kit** and **.NET MAUI** extensions |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) — has built-in MAUI support |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | **Discontinued** — no longer available |

## 7. Create & Verify a Project

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for all targets
dotnet build

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Verification Checklist

```bash
# 1. .NET SDK installed
dotnet --version

# 2. MAUI workload present
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui"

# 3. Xcode CLI tools
xcode-select -p               # should print a path

# 4. iOS simulators available
xcrun simctl list devices available

# 5. Android SDK accessible
dotnet build -t:GetAndroidSdkInfo

# 6. Clean build succeeds
dotnet new maui -n VerifySetup && cd VerifySetup && dotnet build
```

## Version Discovery Tips

- **Latest .NET SDK:** `dotnet sdk check` (after install) or check [dotnet.microsoft.com/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download)
- **Latest MAUI version:** `dotnet workload update` keeps you current
- **Xcode ↔ iOS compatibility:** Apple's [Xcode release notes](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode-release-notes)
- **MAUI ↔ .NET version matrix:** [MAUI release notes on GitHub](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases)

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → (optional) Android Studio → VS Code + extensions. Then `dotnet new maui` and build to verify.
